_description: Effluent constituent properties for OSSF groundwater screening.
_sources: USEPA (1996). Soil Screening Guidance: Technical Background Document. EPA/540/R-95/128.
USEPA (2002). Onsite Wastewater Treatment Systems Manual. EPA/625/R-00/008.
Pang, L. (2009). Microbial removal rates in subsurface media estimated from published studies. Journal of Environmental Quality 38:1531-1559.
Lowe, K.S. et al. (2009). Fate and transport of nutrients, pathogens, and indicator organisms in the subsurface of onsite wastewater systems. EPA/600/R-09/098.
40 CFR Part 141 - National Primary Drinking Water Regulations.

_units.K_oc_mL_per_g: Organic-carbon partition coefficient (mL/g = L/kg); K_d = K_oc * f_oc
_units.lambda_per_day: First-order decay / removal rate constant (1/day); C(t)=C0*exp(-lambda*t)
_units.limit_unit: Units for the limit and for C0 values supplied in the site config

Nitrate-N.notes: Nitrate is not appreciably sorbed to soil. Conservative denitrification rate 0.02/day (aerobic vadose zone). MCL = 10 mg/L as N (40 CFR 141.62). Default C0 = 40 mg/L represents high-end septic tank effluent before any soil treatment.

E. coli.notes: Fecal indicator bacteria. Retardation treated as R=1 (conservative; actual attachment is positive). Removal rate 0.80/day is a conservative median from Pang (2009) for saturated sandy/loamy soils; rates are higher in fine-textured soils. MCL for E. coli = 0 CFU/100 mL in drinking water (TCEQ/EPA). Default C0 = 100 CFU/100 mL represents secondary-treated, disinfected effluent.

Virus.notes: Enteric virus (conservative surrogate). Retardation R=1 (conservative; viruses do sorb to soil, especially at lower pH). Rate 0.30/day is a conservative lower bound from Pang (2009). USEPA GWUDI 4-log removal target applied as limit=0. Default C0 = 10 PFU/L after secondary aerobic treatment + disinfection.

Phosphorus.notes: Total phosphorus. Phosphorus removal in soil is primarily via ligand exchange (not linear Koc sorption); this conservative estimate uses lambda=0 and limit=0.1 mg/L (stream protective standard). A site-specific P-sorption capacity analysis may replace this conservative screening value. Default C0 = 10 mg/L for secondary-treated effluent.
